heading: Fraudulent acceptance of payments
label: 6102 Fraudulent acceptance of payments
notes: Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 3502 of this titlesubstituted “administered by the Secretary” for “administered by the Veterans’ Administration” in subsecs. (a) and (b).Pub. L. 102–83amended section as in effect immediately before the enactment of by substituting “in accordance with title 18” for “not more than $2,000” in subsecs. (a) and (b).Pub. L. 102–54Pub. L. 102–40
amendments: Amendments1991— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 102–40section 3502 of this titlesubstituted “administered by the Secretary” for “administered by the Veterans’ Administration” in subsecs. (a) and (b).Pub. L. 102–83amended section as in effect immediately before the enactment of by substituting “in accordance with title 18” for “not more than $2,000” in subsecs. (a) and (b).Pub. L. 102–54Pub. L. 102–40

children.label: a
children.text: Any person entitled to monetary benefits under any of the laws administered by the Secretary whose right to payment thereof ceases upon the happening of any contingency, who thereafter fraudulently accepts any such payment, shall be fined in accordance with title 18, or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.

children.label: b
children.text: Whoever obtains or receives any money or check under any of the laws administered by the Secretary without being entitled to it, and with intent to defraud the United States or any beneficiary of the United States, shall be fined in accordance with title 18, or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.
